steps.title: Let's build an Open Ended slide!
steps.description: An Open Ended slide lets your audience answer in their own words — no fixed choices, just real responses. We'll set one up together in under a minute. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New presentation' to start a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Open Ended slide type
steps.description: Select 'Open Ended' from the slide type picker. You'll find it in the interactive slides section — it's the one that captures free-text answers from your audience.

steps.title: Write your question
steps.description: Type your open-ended question here. Make it specific enough to spark a real response — 'What's one thing you'd improve about our onboarding?' beats 'What do you think?'

action.value: What's one thing you'd change about our team meetings?

steps.title: Choose how responses display
steps.description: Pick Grid to show all responses at once, or One by one to reveal them individually. Grid works great for spotting patterns; One by one is better for reading each response aloud.

steps.title: Tune your settings
steps.description: Toggle on Time limit to keep answers snappy, Hide results to reveal responses on your schedule, or Allow images so participants can share photos instead of text. Each setting is independent.

mainInteractions.label: open preview via JS click (bypasses overlay backdrop)

steps.title: Audience types their answer here
steps.description: When your audience joins, they type their answer in this box and hit Submit. Responses arrive on your screen the moment they're sent.

steps.title: Responses appear live on your screen
steps.description: Each submission pops up instantly on the presenter view. Hit Present when you're ready to go live — your audience sees the same experience on their phones.
